title: US need support from Europeans in Iran war despite being ‘left out’ of decision-making process
description: Political Journalist John Fund details how the United States and Israel need support from European allies for the war in Iran.   “The Europeans were not consulted about this war; they feel left out of the decision-making process,” Mr Fund told Sky News host Rowan Dean.  “President Trump has made it clear, the Israelis have made it clear, that they are going to continue bombing regardless of what they [Europeans] think.”

title: ‘They like the duopoly’: Other parties do not want the competition of One Nation
description: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce says it will be difficult for One Nation in the South Australian lower house because other parties do not want competition and “like the duopoly”.   “I think you’ll get a proper indication of how One Nation’s going in South Australia’s upper house,” Mr Joyce told Sky News host Rowan Dean.  “It’s going to be difficult for One Nation because the other parties are preferenced to make sure One Nation doesn’t get there, they don’t want competition in the marketplace, they like the duopoly.”

title: ‘Keir is no Winston Churchill’: Trump’s comments reflective of deeper divide between UK and US
description: GB News host Alex Armstrong details how the relationship between the United States and the United Kingdom is fragmenting after US President Donald Trump repeated that UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is “no Winston Churchill”.   “Keir Starmer is no Winston Churchill, those are the latest words from President of the United States Donald Trump,” Mr Armstrong told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “President Trump turned around, pointed at it, and said Keir is no Winston Churchill.”

title: Fresh trouble for former prince Andrew as investigations may widen to corruption charges
description: News Corp Correspondent Sophie Elsworth believes investigations into former prince Andrew could widen to corruption charges.  “We know that he’s [former prince Andrew] already under investigation by Thames Valley Police and many other police forces here in the UK for suspicion of alleged misconduct in public office,” Ms Elsworth told Sky News host Steve Price.  “Now they’re looking at extending this to potential corruption charges.”

title: ‘Very jittery’: UK frustration grows over involvement in Middle East conflict
description: News Corp Correspondent Sophie Elsworth details the current state of affairs in the UK surrounding the Middle East conflict.  “Largely, Britons are frustrated here because, like all around the world, their prices are going up because they’re questioning why they’re involved in this war,” Ms Elsworth told Sky News host Steve Price.  “There’s been polling out here that shows a majority of Britons do not support the UK joining the US in an offensive in the Strait of Hormuz.  “It’s very jittery here at the moment because we know this is an economy that’s already struggling as it is.”

title: SA poll figures show One Nation ‘thrashing’ the Liberals
description: Sky News host Steve Price breaks down the recent polls ahead of the South Australia election on March 21.  “On the poll figures, One Nation is showing a primary vote of 22 per cent compared with the Liberals back on 16 per cent,” Mr Price said.   “One Nation on those poll numbers would win somewhere between 46 to 55 seats and become the main Opposition party.”

title: ‘Dumbest thing he’s done’: Bernardi blasts Malinauskas after bizarre speech
description: One Nation South Australia Candidate Cory Bernardi calls out South Australia Premier Malinauskas for a bizarre speech.  “This is the dumbest thing he’s done all campaign,” Mr Bernardi told Sky News host Caleb Bond.  “Pauline Hanson’s not running for Parliament, I’m running for Parliament for One Nation.”

title: Albanese blasted pandering to the Muslim vote and threatening social cohesion
description: Shadow Home Affairs Minister Jonathon Duniam blasts the Albanese government for failing to protect Australians from threats to national social cohesion.   “They’re the ones who’ve led us to this outcome sitting on the fence,” Mr Duniam told Sky News host Caleb Bond.  “They are very worried about, frankly, the Muslim vote.  “If they don’t court that, they may not win the seats they need ahead of any election.  “It’s been purely political.”

title: Chief economist warns Australia has ‘no spare capacity’ to absorb price adjustments
description: KPMG Australia Chief Economist Brendan Rynne explains what consumers can expect to see as an economic impact from the fuel crisis.  “Any sort of price shock that we have just feeds all the way straight through to inflation,” Mr Rynne told Sky News Business Reporter Ed Boyd.   “There’s no spare capacity to absorb those price adjustments.  “The concern is we're sort of tightly wound up already as an economy.”

title: Global technology stocks smashed after AI replicated their products for free
description: Morningstar Equity Market Strategist Lochlan Halloway breaks down the threat Artificial Intelligence poses to software companies.    “Fear fed on itself, there was just broad-based selling and looking at all software stocks,” Mr Halloway told Sky News Business Reporter Ed Boyd.

title: ‘Serve warm beer’: Britain’s Energy Minister mocked for ridiculous advice to struggling pubs
description: ‘Triggered with Samara Gill’ host Samara Gill mocks UK Energy Minister Ed Miliband for ridiculous advice for pubs financially struggling.   “Britain’s pubs are facing crippling energy bills, prices are soaring, and closures are accelerating,” Ms Gill told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “The big intervention from our Net Zero Czar slash Energy Minister mad Ed Miliband is to serve warmer beer and to turn the fridge off overnight.”

title: ‘Within days society begins to fail’: Uhlmann warns of the stark reality without fuel
description: Sky News Political Contributor Chris Uhlmann explains the impact of the conflict in the Middle East on Australian society.  “This latest Gulf war is a lesson in how the world really works and what fuels it runs on,” Mr Uhlmann told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “Most goods travel by diesel-powered trucks; diesel also drives agriculture and mining, without it, Australia stops.  “Within days of the Trucks stopping, society would begin to fail.  “Where are not there yet, but we are profoundly exposed.”

title: World’s Gone Mad: ‘Certifiably insane’ Candace Owens is now sickened by Trump
description: Sky News host Rowan Dean shamelessly mocks Candace Owens who claims the current war is underway in order to distract attention from Charlie Kirk's real murderer.

title: World’s Gone Mad: Tucker Carlson’s bizarre confession CIA investigated him as a spy
description: Sky News host Rowan Dean mocks right-wing podcasters for going “truly bonkers” after Tucker Carlson’s confession he was being investigated by the CIA for being a spy.

title: ‘Ashamed to be British’: Starmer fails to protect UK troops in Cyprus
description: GB News host Alex Armstrong says UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s decision not to defend British troops in Cyprus has left him “ashamed to be British”.   “That is how it all started,” Mr Armstrong told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “Emmanuel Macron then twisted the knife in Sir Keir's back by sending a battleship to defend a British base in Cyprus … this whole thing has left me ashamed to be British … we’ve also failed to protect British troops.”
